finding_results.notes: Four targeted edits across lines 147-175. (1) Line 147: removed narrator's 'composing, not retreating' pre-authorization of polish; replaced with physical beat showing Lena struggling to start ('opened her mouth once, closed it'). (2) Lines 153-157: broke the monologue into two paragraphs separated by a breathing beat (line 155); replaced 'That isn't exact' with an abandoned sentence 'That isn't —'; added physical grounding gesture; split the description of normalcy into a separate turn with visible self-emphasis ('When I'm actually there'). (3) Line 159: replaced the aphoristic 'The worst part is coming back' with a halting start-stop-restart ('Coming back is —' / Started over); restructured so the diagnosis arrives through searching rather than pre-composed delivery. (4) Line 175: replaced the two-word kicker 'Accurate, but external' and the antithetical compression 'time isn't what goes missing. Continuity does' with more searching speech — abandoned starts ('That's —'), shorter sensory proofs ('Sometimes the water's colder. That's how I know.'), and a concrete ending ('the stroke is mine, the turns are mine, everything looks right. But I was somewhere else') that states the horror plainly rather than packaging it as a literary verdict. All three required facts preserved: motor-functional during desync, undetectable from outside, lost interval returns as external evidence. At least four visible restarts/self-corrections across the revised span.
